title: Lonicera trichosantha alleviates LPS-induced endometritis in mice by modulating the gut microbiota and host metabolism.
publicationName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
journalName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
snippet: Endometritis is an inflammatory disorder of the endometrial lining. Conventional antibiotic therapy often fails to control the accompanying disruptive inflammation. The 95 % ethanol-eluted fraction of Lonicera trichosantha (95 %-LT), exhibits potent anti-inflammatory activity in vitro. Nevertheless, its efficacy in vivo and the mechanisms underlying its pot...
